nodes.context: North American neo-Nazi organization founded in the 1970s, based in Idaho, adhering to Christian Identity.
Christian Identity church founded by Wesley A. Swift, later controlled by Richard Butler.
White supremacist terrorist group formed by Robert Jay Mathews and Aryan Nations members.
Group formed by Dennis McGiffen, inspired by The Order, with ties to Aryan Nations.
Splinter group of Aryan Nations based in New York, later Texas, led by Jay Faber.
Splinter group of Aryan Nations led by Paul Mullet, with branches in 17 states.

nodes.standard_name: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
nodes.original_text_name: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
nodes.context: Enforcement/terrorist wing of Aryan Nations according to the government.
Black nationalist religious organization led by Louis Farrakhan.

edges.evidence_quote: After moving to Idaho, Butler founded Aryan Nations as a paramilitary wing of the church
edges.context: Aryan Nations was founded as the paramilitary wing of the Church of Jesus Christ–Christian.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1983, Robert Jay Mathews, who had visited the Aryan Nations compound many times, formed The Order, along with Aryan Nations members Dan Bauer, Randy Duey, Denver Parmenter, and Bruce Pierce.
edges.context: The Order was formed by Aryan Nations members and had ties to the organization.

edges.evidence_quote: Dennis McGiffen, who also had ties to Aryan Nations, formed a group called 'The New Order', inspired by Mathews' group.
edges.context: The New Order was formed by an individual with ties to Aryan Nations.

edges.evidence_quote: Based in New York, and led by Jay Faber, Aryan Nations Revival became one of the more well-known splinter groups.
edges.context: Aryan Nations Revival is a splinter group of Aryan Nations.

edges.evidence_quote: The most successful splinter group was likely Paul Mullet's Crusaders of Yahweh, which had branches in 17 states.
edges.context: Crusaders of Yahweh is a splinter group of Aryan Nations.

edges.target: Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood
edges.evidence_quote: The government concluded that the Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood was the enforcement/terrorist wing of Aryan Nations.
edges.context: The Holy Order of the Brotherhood of the Phineas Priesthood was considered the enforcement/terrorist wing of Aryan Nations.

edges.evidence_quote: He said that he had a black ally in the plan, Louis Farrakhan, leader of the Nation of Islam.
edges.context: Butler claimed Louis Farrakhan and the Nation of Islam as allies in his plan for racial mini-states.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2005, Kreis received media attention because he attempted to form an Aryan Nations–al Qaeda alliance.
edges.context: August Kreis III attempted to form an alliance between Aryan Nations and al Qaeda.
